Mit uns arbeiten Zusatz-Umsatz erwirtschaftet 1 m EUR Investoren-Kapital, Public Funding und Unternehmensverkäufe gesichert 0 m EUR Unternehmer:innen trainiert und gecoacht 1000 Von komplex zu klar zu umgesetzt. Konsultori arbeitet mit Gründer:innen, KMU und Acceleratoren, um ihre Unternehmen weiterzuentwickeln und zu skalieren. Wir beraten und schulen in den Bereichen Wachstumsstrategie, Investor Readiness, Finanzplanung sowie Exit und M&A – bis zur Umsetzung. Für Acceleratoren konzipieren und liefern wir Trainingsprogramme, die Startup-Teams echte Kompetenz vermitteln. Wir sind selbst erfahrene Unternehmer:innen und bleiben engagiert, bis Ergebnisse erzielt werden. Mehr erfahren Unsere Expertise für Ihr Unternehmen 01. Startups early / mid-stage Wenn Sie Ihr Produkt auf den Markt gebracht haben und über die Anfangsphase hinaus Unterstützung benötigen, unterstützen wir Sie bei Ihren nächsten Schritten. Mehr erfahren 02. Innovative KMUs Wenn Sie ein kleines oder mittleres Unternehmen sind, begleitet Konsultori Sie bei der Stärkung Ihrer Unternehmensziele, Finanzen, Organisation und Entwicklung. Mehr erfahren 03. Investoren Wenn Sie in Start-ups oder KMU investieren und Unterstützung bei der Bewertung ihres Potenzials, der Due-Diligence und der Zusammenarbeit mit ihnen nach Ihrer Investition benötigen, können wir Sie bei Ihrer Entscheidung und der Umsetzung Ihrer Transaktion und neuen Strategie unterstützen. Mehr erfahren 04. Akzeleratoren Wenn Sie einen Accelerator leiten und Experten aus dem Startup-Bereich benötigen, um Ihre Startups zu trainieren und zu coachen oder sogar ein ganzes Startup-Programm anzubieten, stehen wir Ihnen mit unseren Trainern und Live- und digitalen Formaten für Ihre Kohorte zur Seite. Mehr erfahren Ausgezeichnet von Unterstützung für Sie Wählen Sie ein Themengebiet, das am besten zu Ihnen passt, und lassen Sie uns über Ihre Herausforderungen sprechen. Unsere Experten werden Ihre Fragen beantworten und Sie über mögliche Lösungen und Unterstützung beraten. unverbindliches Erstgespräch buchen Wachstum und Strategie M&A - Unternehmensfinanzierung Organisationsdesign Workshops für Akzeleratoren Unsere Partner leiten Ihre Projekte Petra Wolkenstein Gründerin von Konsultori petra.wolkenstein@konsultori.com Michael Kubiena Konsultori Partner michael.kubiena@konsultori.com Unsere Werte Wir sind Unternehmer aus Leidenschaft Unsere Leidenschaft für das Geschäft ist ansteckend. Wir sind selbst Unternehmer und Investoren und wollen unsere Erfahrungen und unser Wissen mit Kunden und Partnern teilen. Wir gehen den ganzen Weg Beratung und Konzepte sind nur der Anfang. Wir geben nicht so schnell auf, krempeln die Ärmel hoch und erzielen Ergebnisse. Innovative Lösungen Mit unseren innovativen Formaten in Beratung und Training finden wir immer die für unsere Kunden passenden, zukunftsweisenden Lösungen. Wir befähigen zu langfristigem Erfolg Wir befähigen Unternehmer, die Fähigkeiten aufzubauen, um ihre Herausforderungen zu meistern und langfristig erfolgreich zu sein. Wir erwarten von den Unternehmern und von uns selbst, dass wir Verantwortung für eine nachhaltige Zukunft übernehmen - für das Unternehmen und darüber hinaus. Wir glauben an die Kraft der Zusammenarbeit Unsere Kreativität und unser Einfallsreichtum liegen darin, Verbindungen zu schaffen (die andere vielleicht nicht sehen können, sei es konzeptionell oder in Form von Kooperationen), die es den Unternehmern ermöglichen, Herausforderungen zu meistern. Wir schätzen und erwarten Vielfalt Offenheit, Fairness und eine menschliche Note. Unser Team bringt unterschiedliche Perspektiven in unsere Arbeit ein. Wir bemühen uns um eine offene und gleichberechtigte Kommunikation und fördern Feedback und faires Verhalten. Alle anzeigen In den vergangenen Wochen hatten wir die Gelegenheit, von erfahrenen Gründer:innen, Expert:innen aus dem Lebensmittelsystem und Startup-Mentor:innen zu lernen, die uns dazu gebracht haben, größer zu denken, schneller zu testen und den Fokus noch stärker auf echte Kundenbedürfnisse zu legen. Von der Auseinandersetzung mit Lebensmittelsystemen und Konsument:innenverhalten bis hin zur Weiterentwicklung unserer Vertriebskanäle, KPIs und Wachstumsstrategie: Jede Session hat uns praxisnahe Erkenntnisse gebracht, die wir direkt auf HealthBlokk anwenden können. Shideh Heravi HealthBlokk Die Zusammenarbeit mit Franziska war großartig; sie war uns in einigen wirklich schwierigen Zeiten eine wertvolle Ansprechpartnerin. Ihr Coaching hat uns geholfen, unsere Markteinführungsstrategie zu verfeinern und unseren Pitch während der Kapitalbeschaffung zu optimieren. Vielen Dank euch allen! Stephan Hofmann Minimist War ein richtig guter Tag – intensiv, klar, motivierend. Jetzt gehen wir den Weg gemeinsam weiter. Ein großes Danke an Petra Wolkenstein, die uns durch den Tag begleitet hat. Stefan Reichlin-Meldegg Gradient Solutions Heute habe ich meine Finanzberatung mit der unglaublichen Petra Wolkenstein begonnen. Petra verfügt über eine seltene Kombination aus unglaublich fundiertem Fachwissen, Geduld und Klarheit, die komplexe Finanzthemen zugänglich und sogar spannend macht. Sie hilft nicht nur bei den Zahlen, sondern auch dabei, das große Ganze Ihrer Mission zu sehen und wie Sie diese wirklich nachhaltig gestalten können. Ich bin fest davon überzeugt, dass sie derzeit die Beste in Wien ist! 🚀 Rita Laila Fussenegger AufAtmenWG Petra war von Anfang an ein wichtiger Teil von Howie. Was als vorübergehendes Executive Coaching begann, um mir bei der Bewältigung der Herausforderungen als Solopreneur zu helfen, hat sich in den letzten zwei Jahren, in denen wir das Unternehmen ausgebaut haben, zu einer vertrauensvollen, langfristigen Partnerschaft entwickelt. Ihre Klarheit, Strukturiertheit und Fähigkeit, Komplexität zu durchschauen, waren in Zeiten hohen Wachstums und hohen Drucks von unschätzbarem Wert. Darüber hinaus spielte Petra eine Schlüsselrolle bei der Gestaltung unserer Finanzstrategie und der Entwicklung eines soliden Finanzplans zur Unterstützung unserer VC-Finanzierungsrunde. Ich bin ihr für ihre beständige Unterstützung sehr dankbar und würde sie jedem Gründer, der sich auf dem komplexen Weg des Aufbaus eines Unternehmens befindet, wärmstens empfehlen. Ewa Lenart Howie Wir schätzen die Erkenntnisse und das Wissen, das uns die herausragenden Experten von Konsultori vermittelt haben. Ihre Ratschläge helfen uns, schneller und intelligenter zu wachsen. Besonderer Dank gilt Petra Wolkenstein, deren Unterstützung und Mentoring uns dazu inspirieren, größer zu denken und Herausforderungen mit Zuversicht anzugehen. Ihre Ratschläge haben bereits entscheidenden Einfluss darauf, wie wir unseren Wachstums

title: The Recursive CEE Forum
descriptionFull:
The Recursive CEE Forum returns to ViennaUP to connect the innovation leaders in the CEE region and strengthen its role in building Europe’s tech future. Last year, 450 founders, investors, ecosystem builders, and policymakers came together, turning cross-border conversations into real connections.

In 2026, we build on that pace with a sharper focus on outcomes: interactive formats, data-driven insights on the region’s innovation performance, and curated opportunities for founders and investors to connect with intent. The forum creates a stronger bridge between CEE and DACH, enabling collaboration across startups, capital, institutions, and technology leaders. Discussions will cover market expansion, capital efficiency, founder tenacity, strategic technologies, and the regulatory landscape shaping innovation across Europe.

Founders shared how working across fragmented markets, established industries, and complex business environments has influenced their approach to building startups. by Teodora Atanasova Featured posts Editor’s Choice Get started with our best stories Is Deep Tech the Way to Save the European Project? by Ana Marija Kostanic "The Czech Republic Could Afford More Ambition" by Teodora Atanasova Why Did Accel, ElevenLabs' And Vercel’s CEO Just Back Polish Co-Founded Hardware Startup? by Ana Marija Kostanic Our Podcast Listen to the latest episodes The Recursive Podcast What Really Happens When You Live With a Humanoid Robot? Can We Safely Live with Humanoid Robots? 🤖 In this fascinating episode of The Recursive Podcast, we sit down with Emily Kate Genatowski, an AI PhD candidate, historian and domestic robotics researcher who provides a unique, hands-on perspective on our future with machines. Unlike most researchers who work in controlled labs, Emily lives full-time with a humanoid robot named Tova (a Unitree G1) in her apartment in Vienna. As the founder of PSL (Proportional Stake Liability), an insurtech startup bridging the liability gap in robotics, Emily’s career is dedicated to uncovering the practical, legal, and social friction points that arise when AI gains a physical body. She moves beyond the "AI hype" to explore how humanoid robotics will realistically intersect with our legal regulations, physical infrastructure, and the daily fabric of our communities. 🧠 Why Watch? Whether you’re a tech optimist, a skeptic, or someone worried about the future of work, this conversation offers a grounded, practical look at the challenges ahead. Emily moves the needle from philosophical fear to practical empowerment, showing us that we are still the architects of the future we want to see. Why Most Companies Are Measuring AI Success Wrong What does AI engineering actually look like inside modern enterprises — and how close are we to truly autonomous software development? In this episode of The Recursive Podcast, we sit down with Karol Przystalski, Chief Data and AI Officer at Exadel, to explore how organizations are moving beyond AI experimentation and into AI-native operations. We discuss the evolution from AI copilots and coding assistants to fully orchestrated engineering systems, why many companies are measuring AI success incorrectly, and how enterprise leaders should think about ROI, productivity, and organizational change in the age of autonomous engineering. Karol also shares insights into Exadel Colleague — an AI-powered engineering teammate designed to support entire software teams—and explains why the future of AI isn't about replacing humans, but helping them focus on higher-value work. In This Episode: 🔹 Why AI adoption is shifting from experimentation to real business value How enterprises have moved beyond AI proof-of-concepts and are now focused on productivity, efficiency, and measurable outcomes. 🔹 The difference between AI-enabled development and AI-native engineering Why coding assistants are only the first step—and how autonomous systems are transforming entire software development lifecycles. 🔹 How to measure AI ROI beyond token usage The metrics that actually matter, including productivity gains, human-equivalent hours saved, accuracy, and business impact. 🔹 The future of autonomous engineering and the role of humans Why AI won't replace software engineers, but will fundamentally change how teams work and what skills will matter most. 🧠 Whether you're an engineering leader, CTO, product executive, or simply curious about the future of software development, this conversation offers a practical look at where enterprise AI is headed next. Where Is Your Old Smartphone? Rethinking the Way We Buy Tech In this episode of The Recursive Podcast, we sit down with Kilian Kaminski, the co-founder of Refurbed, the Vienna-based marketplace that has become a powerhouse in the European circular economy. Kilian shares insights from his transition from Amazon to entrepreneurship and the ongoing battle to establish refurbishment as a standardized global consumption category. A former head of Amazon’s Certified Refurbished program in Germany, Kilian left the corporate giant to revolutionize how we consume. Today, Refurbed has surpassed €2 billion in sales, proving that "rethinking new" is not just a sustainable choice, but a massive business opportunity that sits at the intersection of consumer trust and environmental impact. What we discussed in this episode: ♻️ The limitations of corporate sustainability and the origins of Refurbed’s marketplace model. ♻️ Overcoming the "chicken and egg" problem of acquiring sellers and building consumer trust in non-new products. ♻️ The critical need for standardized European legal definitions and quality criteria for refurbishment. ♻️ Cultural differences in consumption habits and the untapped "gold mine" of 600 million unused devices in European households. ♻️ The impact of the "Right to Repair" directive and the role of the European Refurbishment Association (EUREFAS) in policy lobbying. 🧠 This episode is a must-watch for entrepreneurs interested in marketplace dynamics, sustainability advocates, and anyone curious about how the "Right to Repair" legislation will impact the gadgets we use every day. See all episodes → Previous posts CEE Startup & Tech Weekly News CEE Startup & Tech Weekly: The Baltics Have a New Unicorn The Recursive’s weekly roundup aims to cover key tech developments across Central and Eastern Europe, as well as the growing impact of CEE-born founders on the global stage. title: WSA Global Congress
descriptionFull:
WSA Global Congress – Scaling Digital Solutions. For People & Planet.

Meet the people behind the world’s most impactful digital solutions at the WSA Global Congress in Vienna.

Connect with social entrepreneurs, innovators, policymakers, investors, and ecosystem builders working to scale digital solutions with social impact globally.

Interactive morning sessions let you exchange ideas and gain practical insights on ethical AI, digital sovereignty, and how to successfully scale tech solutions that address real societal and environmental challenges.

In the afternoon, experience the live pitches of the WSA Winners 2025, presenting digital impact solutions from around the world, offering inspiration, partnership opportunities, and real-world use cases for impact at scale.

Join this truly global, multi-stakeholder community and leave with new connections, optimistic perspectives, and concrete ideas to grow your own impact.

Don't forget to check out WSA Community Day, too!

Find out more about the event agenda, speakers and more on our website:

Programme Overview Imprint Privacy Policy WSA Global Congress in Vienna Scaling Digital Solutions: For People & Planet From May 19-22 the WSA Global Congress went back to its roots in Austria. Attendees exchanged ideas and LinkedIn profiles in the bustling Palais Wertheim, the shadow of the world’s oldest ferris wheel in Hotel Superbode, and the extravagant ballroom at Jugendstiltheater. The community showed up for each […] About Awards Mission UN Context WSIS Context Board WSA Team Contact FAQs Participate WSA Award Benefits Timeline Rules Apply Young Innovators Benefits Timeline Rules Apply Partner Awards Winners WSA Winners WSA ix3 Winners’ Stories Winners’ Impact Network National Experts National Experts Expert’s Corner Youth Community Youth Ambassadors Youth Community Jurors Speakers Patrons Partners Strategic Partners Hosting Partners Sponsoring Partners Media Partners Events Global Congress Jury Events EYIF UniVation Innovation Events Partner Events Media News & Events Press Releases Clippings Publications Newsletter WSA Global Congress in Vienna Scaling Digital Solutions: For People & Planet From May 19-22 the WSA Global Congress went back to its roots in Austria. Attendees exchanged ideas and LinkedIn profiles in the bustling Palais Wertheim, the shadow of the world's oldest ferris wheel in Hotel Superbode, and the extravagant ballroom at Jugendstiltheater. The community showed up for each other yet again behind the pitch deck podium and on the dance floor, proving that in this age of corporate consolidation, rampant inequality and AI incentivised layoffs; there is still enormous power in connection, global cooperation, and ethical innovation. As usual, the crowd was a diverse patchwork of faces, some familiar, some new, with representation from almost half of the 182 UN member states. What did this melting pot all have in common? Ambition, ingenuity and the drive to make the world a better place. Topics Agenda Winners Global Champions Key Participants Welcome statement Why Vienna Photos Partners Catalogue Topics Digital Sovereignty vs. AI | Control, Choice, Community Is it possible to have a conference in 2026 without talking about AI? That's a rhetorical question of course. But the WSA community didn't come all the way to Vienna to talk about increasing profit margins at the expense of the middle class. The more important questions were how to keep digital sovereignty, how to build these systems with integrity, and how to keep AI more inclusive. Global Impact Solutions | Built for Real Needs Impact startups are building the solutions we urgently need - tackling climate, inclusion, education, and health - but often struggle to gain funding and support in systems copying Silicon Valley standards. The community shared insider knowledge on how to level the playing field and support founders and their ventures, as well as how to create value for communities, not just shareholders. Building Digital Public Goods | Meeting Community Demands Digital public infrastructure provides access to services, opportunities and participation, however yet many communities, especially in rural or marginalized areas, remain excluded. When essential tools are privately owned or unevenly deployed, digital divides deepen. In this Global Congress, there were a lot of discussions on how to build shared digital foundations that are open, inclusive, ensuring everyone can benefit from and contribute to the digital future. Five tech giants shouldn’t define our digital lives, own our data and choices. It’s time to pause, reflect and reset. From May 19–22, 2026, Vienna proved that Digital without Harm is possible, welcoming 500+ changemakers from 90+ countries. Renowned for its commitment to digital humanism , the city offered the perfect backdrop to explore how technology can be ethical, inclusive, and human-centered. Set within ViennaUP , Central Europe’s largest startup and innovation festival, participants had access to a wide range of events, gatherings, and networking opportunities, connecting with innovators, policymakers, and impact-driven founders from around the world. Vienna is consistently ranked one of the most livable cities in the world, with excellent public transport, vibrant culture, historic architecture, and world-class food and coffee — the congress was the perfect occasion to finally (re)visit this city. Please get your ticket in the external ticket shop Get your Ticket Description Fostering change through real connections. ​The world may feel like it’s going crazy. Instead of complaining, we created FFX – a space for trusted, authentic, and lasting connections. ​We bring together Europe’s leading founders, investors, and leaders to learn from each other, exchange openly, and build relationships that last a lifetime. And yes, this is a women-only event . ​This time, we bundle strengths as we know it's the only path forward for radical change : FFX joins forces with ViennaUp to bring the Austrian and European ecosystems together. Programme Overview Imprint Privacy Policy Female Founders Building Europe's Innovation Economy. With everyone at the table. About Our Offers FF Experience FF Bootcamp Newsletter Newsletter Built by I.Kunev Newsletter Newsletter FEmale Founders Female Founders is Europe’s leading platform for women shaping the future of business, capital, tech and innovation. Explore our formats Explore our formats Partner with us Partner with us 250+ Startups backed 500+VCs in the Network 50% Deep Tech Partners we've worked with Female Founders isn't here to fit into the tech ecosystem, it's here to rewrite it. A decade ago, two founders saw the systems shaping entrepreneurship weren't built for women. So they built new ones. Today, we connect female founders, investors and corporates across Europe to build stronger companies, better products and a more competitive economy. Women are not a niche. They are one of the most powerful economic forces in the world. Together, we make waves. For Founders Build stronger. Scale faster. Never alone. Female Founders gives women building startups the leverage to win: direct access to capital, corporates, knowledge, operators, investors and a network that moves with them. We strengthen founders with what the market too often withholds, so they move faster, build with conviction, and shape markets that drive a more competitive Europe. From access to capital. From capital to scale. Join as Founder Join as Founder For Partners Reach the women shaping the market. Partners reach the audience defining what gets built, trusted and bought next and turn that proximity into market position, growth and measurable return. A strategic investment in relevance, growth and revenue. Become a Partner Become a Partner For Investors Access the overlooked upside in Europe’s innovation economy. Female Founders connects Business Angels, VCs, CVCs and Family Offices with high-potential startups built and led by women and diverse teams across Europe. We give investors access to opportunities others miss, and to the companies driving Europe's next wave of growth, alongside the co-investors needed to move early. The leverage: sharper market insight, dealflow & stronger networks. Join as Investor Join as Investor Three levers . One community . What we do beneath every founder we back, every investor we connect, every market we move. SUPPORTING THE FOUNDERS We invest in the person before the company. Capability, confidence, clarity. When founders get stronger, everything they build gets stronger with them. CONNECTING THE ECOSYSTEM We make the connections the market leaves to chance. Founders, investors, corporates - all in one place. 87k+ members, 500+ VCs, 175+ partner companies. Relationships that create real economic activity. ENABLING THE ECONOMY We change who builds and who benefits. Hardware, deep tech, infrastructure. Female founders are the untapped majority of Europe's innovation capacity. We are changing that at scale. Road to Global x Tech Summit brings together cultures, industries, opportunities, and meaningful connections in a dynamic two-day program built to accelerate international expansion. Following last year’s milestone of over $20 million in signed deals, this event once again connects you with global partners, new markets, and high-impact networks. Learn how leaders succeeded, where they failed, and what comes next. Across Europe, many events may look similar on the surface yet the world is being reshaped at an unprecedented pace. In this rapidly evolving landscape, remaining confined to a single local market is often the greatest barrier to growth. The path forward is clear: expand into new markets, build international partnerships, and extend your boundaries beyond borders. This year, we are building a far stronger bridge between Europe and Asia. Road to Global, born in Vienna, has become a trusted meeting point for partners and ecosystems from dozens of countries. Tech Summit, one of Asia’s leading innovation platforms, has enabled major cross-border collaborations across multiple markets. Now, these two powerful platforms are joining forces to offer you something greater. At Road to Global x Tech Summit, you will discover concrete pathways to international expansion and form high-value global partnerships that can transform your growth trajectory. If you are ready to move beyond limits and step into global markets, we look forward to welcoming you in Vienna. title: PeaceTech Conference: Advancing Ethical AI in a Sustainable World
descriptionFull:
Innovate. Network. Impact.

It's time to move beyond discussion and start building. Join the PeaceTech Conference 2026 to integrate cutting-edge business strategy with meaningful, next-generation impact.

This year we will explore practical strategies to Advance Ethical AI in a Sustainable World, addressing AI's dual environmental impact: leveraging it for the circular economy while managing data centre footprints. 

From panel discussions and networking opportunities to startup booths and pitching competitions, PTC26 has got you covered.

Whether you are a founder, a developer, or a future industry leader, this is your chance to ensure AI drives a more sustainable world.

The PeaceTech Attendee Pass grants you full access to a day of high-stakes dialogue and innovation at the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and global sustainability. Your experience begins with a visionary keynote on the New Global Compact , followed by a deep dive into our three critical content tracks: The Promise: Explore how AI can power a sustainable future. The Peril: Navigate the geopolitical risks of the resource race. The Policy : Debate the accountability gap in the global environmental price of AI. title: HOIV FUSION: The Beat of Innovation
location: HOIV – Home of Innovation, Arsenalstraße 11, 1030 Vienna
descriptionFull:
Where Tech, Capital, and Culture Collide. Skip the hype. Get to the deals.

Building on our successful Grand Opening at ViennaUP 2025, we will host a dynamic happening at our hub that merges creativity, technology, and investment into a tangible, experience-driven format.

We are transforming the HOIV hub into the ultimate arena for founders, VCs, and tech builders to connect and close.

Why You Need to Be There:

  • Direct Access: Skip the gatekeepers. Meet face-to-face with investors and industry leaders across our stages, studios, and networking zones.

  • Tangible Action: Dive into curated workshops, rapid-fire pitches, and live music-tech performances.

  • The Full Experience: Navigate interactive tech installations, outdoor relaxation areas, a dynamic food court, and an ViennaUP WarmUP Afterparty and Vernissage.

This is where capital meets creation. Stop talking about innovation and come build it.

Tickets Visitor For anyone looking to explore, connect, and experience the energy of ViennaUP at HOIV. Move freely through showcases, panels, installations, and networking areas, and engage with founders, creatives, and technologists throughout the day. FREE TICKET Startup For startups eager to connect, explore, and engage with the innovation community. Join the HOIV Fusion program to meet investors, founders, creatives, and technologists, exchange ideas, and discover opportunities across technology, business, and the arts. FREE TICKET Investor For investors and VCs looking to discover new projects and connect with founders in a dynamic environment. Engage in conversations, explore emerging ideas, and build relationships across technology, business, and creative industries. FREE TICKET Media For journalists and content creators capturing innovation, creativity, and culture. Access a dynamic environment filled with live moments, conversations, and interactive experiences throughout the HOIV Fusion program. FREE TICKET Ecosystem For ecosystem players, partners, and organizations connecting across industries. Engage with startups, investors, and creatives, explore collaboration opportunities, and be part of an open and interactive environment. FREE TICKET Description Where Tech, Capital, and Culture Collide. Skip the hype. Get to the deals. Building on our successful Grand Opening at ViennaUP 2025, we will host a dynamic festival at our hub that merges creativity, technology, and investment into a tangible, experience-driven format . We are transforming the HOIV hub into the ultimate arena for founders, VCs, and tech builders to connect and close. Why You Need to Be Here: Direct Access: Skip the gatekeepers. Meet face-to-face with investors and industry leaders across our stages, studios, and networking zones. Tangible Action: Dive into curated workshops, rapid-fire pitches , and live music-tech performances . The Full Experience: Navigate interactive tech installations, outdoor relaxation areas , a dynamic food court, and an ViennaUP Warm-Up Afterparty and Vernissage . This is where capital meets creation. Stop talking about innovation and come build it. Agenda All-Day: HOIV registered ViennaUP participants will have a chance to sit down, relax and enjoy the space work within HOIV ViennaUp Workspace . Afternoon: The Innovation Showcase From lunchtime, continuing from our morning HOIV Founder Huddle session, the focus is on exploration and interaction, transforming our hub into a living showcase: Program: Interactive Tech & Art: We will feature hands-on VR and 3D Lab experiences including our VR racing challenge finale that allow guests to engage directly with emerging technologies. Live Creativity: The centerpiece will be an interactive audio-visual art exhibition, featuring live painting and digital art creation, making the creative process a visible and engaging part of the event. High-Impact Panels: The afternoon will host focused discussions on critical topics, including an Investor Panel on current market trends and an "Art Meets Business" Panel exploring investment from family offices and new business models for the creative industries. Build It or Kill it session: Founders present a product idea or strategic direction to a live panel of experts for immediate evaluation. Through structured discussion and expert feedback, each concept is assessed to determine whether it should be built further or refined. Open House & Hub Tours: Attendees can tour our facilities, meet resident startups and artists in their studios, and network in our expansive indoor and outdoor spaces, complete with food trucks and a chill-out atmosphere. Evening: The HOIV Fusion Finale Instead of a traditional closing panel, the evening will transition into a high-concept art and networking event. This allows us to end the day with an experience true to our mission, bringing investors, founders, and creatives together in a relaxed, culturally rich setting for meaningful conversations, accompanied by music from our in-house DJs and a vernissage featuring art pieces from our HOIV artists. Accessibility Wheel-chair accessible venue 20.05.2026 12:30 - 19:30 HOIV – Home of Innovation, Arsenalstraße 11, 1030 Vienna Please note that photos and video may be taken during the event for documentation or promotion purposes. Programme Overview Imprint Privacy Policy

title: Creative Days Vienna 2026
descriptionFull:
How are digital technologies shaping the future of cultural experiences? On 20 and 21 May 2026, Creative Days Vienna will once again bring together leading international creative professionals for a diverse program of talks, tours, performances, installations and networking formats.

The participants of Creative Days Vienna 2026 will provide substantial impulses for architecture, design, film, gaming, fashion, music and the visual arts. Together, we will discuss the interplay between technological innovations, social change and the creative industries.

Creative Days Vienna 2026 will open with an (Un)Conference exploring how creatives can reshape cultural and digital infrastructures. The opening features keynotes by Shumon Basar and Günseli Yalcinkaya, as well as performances, installations and a club night. Day 2 brings workshops led by Paloma Oliveira, Ramon Reichert, NeverAtHome, Subotron, Marlène Delrive, Area 404 and a Tour presenting Biofabrique Vienna and Creatives for Vienna projects.

Let’s talk Kreativwirtschaft. Die internationale Kreativszene hat untersucht, wie digitale Technologien kulturelle Erfahrungen neu prägen. Bei den Creative Days Vienna 2026 drehten sich die Diskussionen um das Zusammenspiel von technologischer Innovation, gesellschaftlichem Wandel und den Kreativindustrien. Das Programm vereinte Perspektiven aus den Bereichen Architektur, Design, Film, Gaming, Mode, Musik und bildende Kunst. Es zeigte auf, wie digitale Infrastrukturen und Plattformen kreative Arbeit verändern. Eröffnet wurde die Veranstaltung am 20. Mai mit einer (Un)Conference im Funkhaus Wien , die sich damit befasste, wie Kreativschaffende kulturelle und digitale Infrastrukturen neu gestalten können. Beiträge von Nadia Piet (AIxDESIGN), Calum Bowden (trust) und Christie Morgan (SOFTER) griffen alternative Praktiken, kollaborative Systeme und neue Modelle für digitale Zukünfte auf. Die Content Vienna Screenings von Total View und Merve Sahin präsentierten Projekte, die mithilfe digitaler Werkzeuge verborgene Ebenen urbaner Räume und sozialer Strukturen sichtbar machen. Die Eröffnung wurde durch eine Keynote von Shumon Basar und einen Talk von Günseli Yalcinkaya abgerundet, begleitet von der interaktiven Installation AGENTIC EI von NODE Media Lab sowie einer Performance von Cat Jiménez, kuratiert von NeverAtHome , gefolgt von Networking und einer Clubnacht. Der zweite Tag konzentrierte sich auf Workshops und Austauschformate mit Paloma Oliveira und Kristina Maurer, Ramon Reichert mit NeverAtHome, Marlène Delrive und Georg Russegger mit SUBOTRON , Bika Rebek sowie Klimentina Milenova und Brooklyn J. Pakathi (AREA[404]). Die Sessions behandelten Themen wie neue Formen der Zusammenarbeit, digitale Infrastrukturen und sich wandelnde kreative Ökonomien. Ergänzt wurde das Programm durch eine Tour der Biofabrique Vienna und ausgewählten Projekten von Creatives for Vienna , die aktuelle Initiativen und räumliche Praktiken in der Stadt sichtbar machte. Die Creative Days Vienna 2026 zeigten, dass digitale Technologien nicht nur künstlerische Produktion und Distribution beeinflussen, sondern auch neue Möglichkeiten für Zusammenarbeit, Partizipation und kulturelle Infrastrukturen eröffnen. title: The Future of Health and Age Tech: Pitch Session
descriptionFull:
The Future of Health and Age Tech - Pitch Session

Ready to see what’s next in healthcare and age tech? Join us for The Future of Health and Age Tech - Pitch Session, where the most exciting new startups take the stage. After the pitches we’re hosting a networking session designed to bring everyone together. Whether you are an investor looking for the next big thing, an industry expert, or just passionate about health and age tech innovation, this is your chance to chat, swap ideas, and build lasting partnerships in a relaxed atmosphere.

Come for the inspiration, stay for the people! We can't wait to see you there!

title: Fuckup Nights Vienna Vol 52 | ViennaUP 2026 Edition & Party
descriptionFull:
Fuckup Nights ViennaUP 2026: Burn the Script

Last year was just the warm-up. If you thought we went hard before, you haven't seen anything yet. We’re back at ViennaUP to tear down the wall of perfect entrepreneurship and show you the fuckups, blood, sweat, and bad decisions hidden behind the greatest wins.

We are not a polite panel but rather a riot European Entrepreneurship desperately needs. Forget the sanitized lessons learned slides. We’re bringing three fearless speakers to the stage to bleed out the truth and share their most powerful fuckups & lessons learned.

For this very special occasion, we’ll be taking over the legendary U4 - the home of Falco, birthplace of Viennese counter-culture and now one of the ViennaUP event venues.

Come join us, coz this is where the coolest crowd of the entire ViennaUP week gathers to ditch the masks and get real.

Fail loud. Fail proud. Burn the rulebook.

Come join us, coz this is where the coolest crowd of the entire ViennaUP week gathers to ditch the masks and get real. Fail loud. Fail proud. Burn the rulebook. Agenda 5:30 PM - 6:30 PM: Doors Open, Networking Warm-up, Drinks 6:30 PM - 8:00 PM: Fuckup Stories + Q&A – Good things take time, so expect us to run a little over 😆 8:00 PM - 10:00 PM: Afterparty & Networking with Drinks & Music What to expect: • Honest stories of failure from entrepreneurs, innovators, and dreamers • Lessons learned that will spark your own journey forward • Networking with the boldest and bravest in the Fuckup Nights + ViennaUP community • Good vibes and a celebration of resilience Accessibility Visual aids or descriptions for visually impaired attendees 20.05.2026 17:30 - 22:00 U4 Vienna, Schönbrunnerstr. 222-228, 1120 Vienna Please note that photos and video may be taken during the event for documentation or promotion purposes. Programme Overview Imprint Privacy Policy Fuckup Nights Join the global movement of Fuckup Nights, where entrepreneurs share their failures and learnings at engaging events. SOMETIMES WE WIN. SOMETIMES WE LEARN. FUCKUP NIGHTS Home Eventkalender The Failure Institute Featured in Über uns More Home Eventkalender The Failure Institute Featured in Über uns Sign In Create Account My Account Signed in as: filler@godaddy.com My Account Sign out SUBSCRIBE Signed in as: filler@godaddy.com Home Eventkalender The Failure Institute Featured in Über uns Account My Account Sign out Sign In My Account SUBSCRIBE FUCKUP NIGHTS FUCKUP NIGHTS FUCKUP NIGHTS FUCKUP NIGHTS SOMETIMES WE WIN. SOMETIMES WE LEARN. ZUM EVENTKALENDER FUCKUP NIGHTS FÜR UNTERNEHMEN FUCKUP NIGHTS FUCKUP NIGHTS FUCKUP NIGHTS FUCKUP NIGHTS SOMETIMES WE WIN. SOMETIMES WE LEARN. ZUM EVENTKALENDER FUCKUP NIGHTS FÜR UNTERNEHMEN Falls du uns irgendwoher kennst, vielleicht von hier. FAILURE CULTURE EATS STRATEGY FOR BREAKFAST Die Fuckup Nights sind eine globale Bewegung bei der berufliche Misserfolge geteilt, hinterfragt und die Lerneffekte daraus zelebriert werden. Bei jeder Veranstaltung haben wir drei Sprecher:innen, welche in genau 10 Minuten ihre Fuckups, aber vor allem auch ihre Lessons Learned präsentieren. Die allererste Fuckup Night in Österreich hat 2014 in Wien stattgefunden. Heute gibt es die Fuckup Nights in ganz Österreich und weltweit mehr als 80 Ländern. Wir sind stolz darauf, "the most active entrepreneurs and creators movement on the planet" zu sein, denn wir alle verfolgen die Vision, Scheitern politisch, gesellschaftlich und persönlich zu entstigmatisieren und stehen für einen Mindset, der zum Erfolg führt. FUCKUP NIGHTS THE MOVEMENT Die Fuckup Nights werden weltweit von verschiedenen Community-Leaderinnen und -Leadern entwickelt und durchgeführt, die nicht zwingend miteinander verbunden sind. Auch unsere Fuckup Nights in Wien, OÖ, K, NÖ (und bald auch dem Bgld) sind nicht nur Teil dieser großartigen Bewegung – sie sind die Bewegung – werden jedoch komplett selbstständig und unabhängig organisiert und stehen in keiner wirtschaftlichen Verbindung zu den Organisationen und Veranstaltungen in anderen Ländern. title: ViennaUP Coffee House Sessions 2026
descriptionFull:
Step into the Viennese founder spirit at the ViennaUP Coffee House Sessions 2026! In hand-picked, invitation-style round tables, selected startups meet experienced investors and industry experts for honest conversations, sharp questions, and connections that can move your venture forward.

Expect curated matchmaking by the Vienna Business Agency team after registration to ensure the right mix of people and topics—so every seat at the table matters. All of this happens in iconic Viennese coffee houses, where big ideas and bold decisions have always belonged.

If you’re fundraising, scaling, or simply looking for the right network and strategic sparring partners, this is your moment. Apply now and secure your chance to join the Coffee House Sessions at ViennaUP 2026.

Tickets Startup Application After you submit your application and choose your preferred host, our team will review all submissions and curate the table line-up (limited seats). We will get back to you via email with the result. FREE TICKET Description Step into the Viennese founder spirit at the ViennaUP Coffee House Sessions 2026 : invitation-style round tables where selected startups meet experienced investors and innovation managers for formal conversations, sharp questions, and connections that can move your venture forward. After registering, you’ll be asked to apply for one of three table hosts (Investor / Innovation Manager). The Vienna Business Agency team will then curate the final line-up to ensure the right mix of startups and topics at each table. Capacity is limited: 3 startups per host per session, so every seat at the table matters. This format is designed for startups that are beyond the early idea stage—typically with validated traction (e.g., MVP in market, first revenues, growth signals, or a clear scaling/fundraising roadmap). If you’re fundraising, scaling, or looking for strategic sparring and the right network, this is your moment. Sessions take place May 18–22 in morning and afternoon time slots, hosted in iconic Viennese coffee houses—where big ideas and bold decisions have always belonged. Apply now to secure your chance to join the Coffee House Sessions at ViennaUP 2026. The application will be open until the 4th of May 11:00 am. We will get back to you after the application deadline. When applying, please select your preferred host and briefly share your traction, goals, and what you want to discuss at the table. title: Road to Global x Tech Summit
descriptionFull:
Two major platforms unite to deliver a broader, more inclusive experience designed for global growth. Road to Global x Tech Summit brings together cultures, industries, opportunities, and meaningful connections in a dynamic two-day program built to accelerate international expansion. Following last year’s milestone of over $20 million in signed deals, this event once again connects you with global partners, new markets, and high-impact networks. Learn how leaders succeeded, where they failed, and what comes next.

Please get your ticket in the external ticket shop Get your Ticket Description Two major platforms unite to deliver a broader, more inclusive experience designed for global growth. Road to Global x Tech Summit brings together cultures, industries, opportunities, and meaningful connections in a dynamic two-day program built to accelerate international expansion. Following last year’s milestone of over $20 million in signed deals, this event once again connects you with global partners, new markets, and high-impact networks. Learn how leaders succeeded, where they failed, and what comes next. Across Europe, many events may look similar on the surface yet the world is being reshaped at an unprecedented pace. In this rapidly evolving landscape, remaining confined to a single local market is often the greatest barrier to growth. The path forward is clear: expand into new markets, build international partnerships, and extend your boundaries beyond borders. This year, we are building a far stronger bridge between Europe and Asia. Road to Global, born in Vienna, has become a trusted meeting point for partners and ecosystems from dozens of countries. Tech Summit, one of Asia’s leading innovation platforms, has enabled major cross-border collaborations across multiple markets. Now, these two powerful platforms are joining forces to offer you something greater. At Road to Global x Tech Summit, you will discover concrete pathways to international expansion and form high-value global partnerships that can transform your growth trajectory. If you are ready to move beyond limits and step into global markets, we look forward to welcoming you in Vienna. title: Best practices for expanding your business to Vienna
descriptionFull:
Are you planning to start a business in Vienna and want to learn from people who’ve already done it?

At this event, you will meet founders who have been supported by the Vienna Business Agency and are ready to share their experience of building a business in Vienna. They will provide practical insights and highlight the key topics to consider when founding a company — from the first steps and funding options to legal requirements and networking opportunities.

Participants will also have the chance to ask questions, exchange ideas, and connect with Vienna’s entrepreneurial community.

title: Onco:Innovate Conference
location: Future Health Lab, Alfred-Adler-Straße 1, 1100, Vienna, Austria
descriptionFull:
Onco:Innovate returns to Vienna for its second flagship edition.

The format is a multidisciplinary conference at the crossroads of a high-level oncology symposium and a dynamic startup & innovation summit. Onco:Innovate highlights emerging trends and groundbreaking solutions in oncology, addresses barriers to market entry, and fosters impactful partnerships in the fight against cancer. We unite leading experts from across the international oncology ecosystem, including startups & scaleups, hospitals & clinics, pharma & corporates, patient organizations, policymakers, payers, NGOs, and investors. From medical breakthroughs and precision therapies to AI-driven solutions, funding models, regulatory strategies, and project consortia, Onco:Innovate elevates the different perspectives to improve access, quality of life, and outcomes. This is where collaboration transforms innovation into better cancer care. Join us and help shape the future of oncology.

15:00:00 35550 1,2,6,9 false 0 09:00:00 1,2,5,6 1771594796 273 ViennaUP | Onco:Innovate Conference Programme Log in Onco:Innovate Conference To book a ticket please kindly register or log in here . Tickets Onco:Innovate Ticket For our Onco:Innovate Friends & Partners FREE TICKET Description Onco:Innovate is a multidisciplinary conference at the intersection of a high-level oncology conference and a modern startup & innovation event. The conference focuses on highlighting trends in oncology, overcoming barriers to market entry and creating impactful partnerships in the fight against cancer. We bring together leading experts from across the oncology ecosystem, including innovators, hospitals, pharma & corporates, patient organizations, policymakers, payers, NGOs and investors, to spotlight emerging trends, address barriers to market access, and build partnerships that accelerate the adoption of impactful cancer innovations. From medical breakthroughs and precision therapies to digital and AI-driven solutions, funding models, and regulatory strategies, Onco:Innovate amplifies the patient perspective to improve access, quality of life, and outcomes. This is where collaboration turns innovation into better cancer care. Join us and help shape the future of oncology. Agenda From Breakthroughs to Bottlenecks - When oncology innovation moves faster than access and administration. Strategies to bridge the gap between discovery and delivery. AI & Digital Transformation in Cancer Care: How AI and digital technologies are enabling more precise, efficient, and patient-centred oncology solutions. Collaborating for Impact: Building Networks, Ecosystems, and Funding Pathways in Oncology - Sharing insights on creating partnerships and consortia that foster innovation, accelerate research, and support sustainable oncology solutions. Current Oncology Market Dynamics, Challenges & Solutions - Exploring market trends, challenges, and strategies for effectively navigating the European oncology landscape to optimize innovation adoption. Policy, Regulation & Market Access: Strategies & Best Practice - Analyzing policy frameworks, regulatory pathways, and market access strategies to ensure innovative oncology treatments reach patients efficiently. Funding Models for Oncology Innovators - Exploring how investors support the development and commercialization of breakthrough oncology technologies. Disruptive Innovation in Oncology: Precision Therapies and Next-Gen Diagnostics - Examining emerging therapies, precision medicine, and cutting-edge diagnostic tools that are transforming the way cancer is detected and treated. Prevention, Patient-Centric Care & Quality of Life - Highlighting approaches to improve patient outcomes, enhance quality of life, and emphasize preventive care in modern oncology practices. Oncology Innovation Demo Day - Presentation of market ready innovative solutions, startups and scaleups Accessibility Wheel-chair accessible venue 21.05.2026 09:00 - 15:00 Future Health Lab, Alfred-Adler-Straße 1, 1100 Wien, Austria Please note that photos and video may be taken during the event for documentation or promotion purposes. Programme Overview Imprint Privacy Policy Onco:Innovate | European Oncology Innovation Accelerator Onco:Innovate is Europe's oncology innovation accelerator and investor network -- connecting cancer startups, investors, and oncology experts in Vienna. Home About us Activities Events Reports Austria 2026 Oncology Innovation Report Onco Accelerator Campaigns News Join Contact ✕ We are the European Oncology Innovation Network ONCO:INNOVATE CLOSING THE GAP FROM INNOVATION TO MARKET Onco:Innovate is an international initiative committed to closing the gap between oncology innovation and market adoption. Our mission is to foster strategic collaboration among key stakeholders, accelerate the time-to-market for ground-breaking solutions, and ensure that patients benefit more quickly from the latest scientific advancements. By connecting startups, healthcare providers, corporates, investors, and public institutions, we drive the development and implementation of transformative cancer care solutions. Through year-round programming, we actively support the journey of oncology innovations from lab to bedside. title: WSA Global Congress
descriptionFull:
WSA Global Congress – Scaling Digital Solutions. For People & Planet.

Meet the people behind the world’s most impactful digital solutions at the WSA Global Congress in Vienna.

Connect with social entrepreneurs, innovators, policymakers, investors, and ecosystem builders working to scale digital solutions with social impact globally.

Interactive morning sessions let you exchange ideas and gain practical insights on ethical AI, digital sovereignty, and how to successfully scale tech solutions that address real societal and environmental challenges.

In the afternoon, experience the live pitches of the WSA Winners 2025, presenting digital impact solutions from around the world, offering inspiration, partnership opportunities, and real-world use cases for impact at scale.

Join this truly global, multi-stakeholder community and leave with new connections, optimistic perspectives, and concrete ideas to grow your own impact.

Don't forget to check out WSA Community Day, too!

Find out more about the event agenda, speakers and more on our website:

Programme Overview Imprint Privacy Policy WSA Global Congress in Vienna Scaling Digital Solutions: For People & Planet From May 19-22 the WSA Global Congress went back to its roots in Austria. Attendees exchanged ideas and LinkedIn profiles in the bustling Palais Wertheim, the shadow of the world’s oldest ferris wheel in Hotel Superbode, and the extravagant ballroom at Jugendstiltheater. The community showed up for each […] About Awards Mission UN Context WSIS Context Board WSA Team Contact FAQs Participate WSA Award Benefits Timeline Rules Apply Young Innovators Benefits Timeline Rules Apply Partner Awards Winners WSA Winners WSA ix3 Winners’ Stories Winners’ Impact Network National Experts National Experts Expert’s Corner Youth Community Youth Ambassadors Youth Community Jurors Speakers Patrons Partners Strategic Partners Hosting Partners Sponsoring Partners Media Partners Events Global Congress Jury Events EYIF UniVation Innovation Events Partner Events Media News & Events Press Releases Clippings Publications Newsletter WSA Global Congress in Vienna Scaling Digital Solutions: For People & Planet From May 19-22 the WSA Global Congress went back to its roots in Austria. Attendees exchanged ideas and LinkedIn profiles in the bustling Palais Wertheim, the shadow of the world's oldest ferris wheel in Hotel Superbode, and the extravagant ballroom at Jugendstiltheater. The community showed up for each other yet again behind the pitch deck podium and on the dance floor, proving that in this age of corporate consolidation, rampant inequality and AI incentivised layoffs; there is still enormous power in connection, global cooperation, and ethical innovation. As usual, the crowd was a diverse patchwork of faces, some familiar, some new, with representation from almost half of the 182 UN member states. What did this melting pot all have in common? Ambition, ingenuity and the drive to make the world a better place. Topics Agenda Winners Global Champions Key Participants Welcome statement Why Vienna Photos Partners Catalogue Topics Digital Sovereignty vs. AI | Control, Choice, Community Is it possible to have a conference in 2026 without talking about AI? That's a rhetorical question of course. But the WSA community didn't come all the way to Vienna to talk about increasing profit margins at the expense of the middle class. The more important questions were how to keep digital sovereignty, how to build these systems with integrity, and how to keep AI more inclusive. Global Impact Solutions | Built for Real Needs Impact startups are building the solutions we urgently need - tackling climate, inclusion, education, and health - but often struggle to gain funding and support in systems copying Silicon Valley standards. The community shared insider knowledge on how to level the playing field and support founders and their ventures, as well as how to create value for communities, not just shareholders. Building Digital Public Goods | Meeting Community Demands Digital public infrastructure provides access to services, opportunities and participation, however yet many communities, especially in rural or marginalized areas, remain excluded. When essential tools are privately owned or unevenly deployed, digital divides deepen. In this Global Congress, there were a lot of discussions on how to build shared digital foundations that are open, inclusive, ensuring everyone can benefit from and contribute to the digital future. Five tech giants shouldn’t define our digital lives, own our data and choices. It’s time to pause, reflect and reset. From May 19–22, 2026, Vienna proved that Digital without Harm is possible, welcoming 500+ changemakers from 90+ countries. Renowned for its commitment to digital humanism , the city offered the perfect backdrop to explore how technology can be ethical, inclusive, and human-centered. Set within ViennaUP , Central Europe’s largest startup and innovation festival, participants had access to a wide range of events, gatherings, and networking opportunities, connecting with innovators, policymakers, and impact-driven founders from around the world. Vienna is consistently ranked one of the most livable cities in the world, with excellent public transport, vibrant culture, historic architecture, and world-class food and coffee — the congress was the perfect occasion to finally (re)visit this city. title: Smart City SuMMit 2026
location: Climate Lab, Spittelauer Lände 45, 8th floor, 1090 Vienna
descriptionFull:
Smart City SuMMit 2026: Let’s Co-Create Circular Cities!

Digitalization is more than a buzzword—it is a catalyst for a circular urban economy. At the Smart City SuMMit 2026, we are moving beyond theory to focus on digital solutions for circular cities that work. Our mission is to bridge the gap between high-level strategy and real-world implementation by connecting impactful innovators with bold industry leaders.

Why Join the SuMMit?

  • High-Speed Impact: We skip the small talk. Our curated kick-off ensures you meet relevant partners in the very first slot.
  • Reverse Pitches: Hear directly from "solution seekers" about the real-world innovation challenges that need to be solved now.
  • Marketplace of Solutions: Discover around 15 hand-picked solution providers showcasing digital solutions for the circular economy.
  • Funding : Gain insights from funding agency experts on funding and scaling circular solutions in Vienna.

Who Should Join?

We invite business leaders, industry experts, decision-makers, and entrepreneurs to discover market-ready solutions and initiate high-impact partnership

title: Manufacturing Day
descriptionFull:
The Manufacturing Day is the leading event for the manufacturing industry during ViennaUP. It brings together more than 500 startups, key industrial players, investors, researchers and experts from 40+ different countries. The event features startup and corporate pitching sessions, inspiring keynotes and discussions with renowned global speakers, matchmaking and networking opportunities, and showcases the latest innovations and technologies from European research. 

Manufacturing Day 2026 focuses on one of the most critical challenges for the global industrial economy: how to successfully scale manufacturing ventures into competitive, resilient and globally operating companies. Manufacturing innovation is thriving, yet many startups struggle to scale from pilot to global production. Manufacturing Day 2026 explores what enables successful industrial scale-up, examining investment models, policy frameworks and partnerships that turn innovation into impact.

FREE TICKET Description The Manufacturing Day is the leading event for the manufacturing industry during ViennaUP. It brings together more than 500 startups, key industrial players, investors, researchers and experts from 50+ different countries. The event features startup and corporate pitching sessions, inspiring keynotes and discussions with renowned global speakers, matchmaking and networking opportunities, and showcases the latest innovations and technologies from European research. Manufacturing Day 2026 focuses on one of the most critical challenges for the global industrial economy: how to successfully scale manufacturing ventures into competitive, resilient and globally operating companies . Manufacturing innovation is thriving, yet many startups struggle to scale from pilot to global production. Manufacturing Day 2026 explores what enables successful industrial scale-up, examining investment models, policy frameworks and partnerships that turn innovation into impact. Agenda 09:30 - 10:30 Admission 10:30 - 12:30 Pitching - Startups & Corporates 12:30 - 14:00 Networking Lunch & Matchmaking 14:00 - 16:30 Investing in Europe's Manufacturing Future - Keynotes & Panel Discussion 16:30 - 20:00 Networking & Matchmaking Accessibility Wheel-chair accessible venue 21.05.2026 09:30 - 20:00 Novotel Wien Hauptbahnhof, Canettistrasse 6, 1100 Vienna, Austria Please note that photos and video may be taken during the event for documentation or promotion purposes. Programme Overview Imprint Privacy Policy Home | Manufacturing Day 2026 The Manufacturing Day is the leading event for the manufacturing industry during ViennaUP, the largest decentralised startup festival in Europe. Manufacturing Day 2026 Manufacturing Day 2026 21 May 2026 | Vienna, Austria Home Agenda Project Expo Sponsors Speakers Log in Register Log in Register Log in Register Info Keynote Speaker Startup Award Startups Infineon Startup Challenge De Nora Startup Challenge About ViennaUp Manufacturing Day 2026 21 May 2026 | 1100 Vienna, Austria Canettistrasse 6 Novotel Wien Hauptbahnhof Share Registration ended on 21 May 2026 This year's topic: Invest to scale. Manufacturing Day 2026 focuses on one of the most critical challenges for the global industrial economy: how to successfully scale manufacturing ventures into competitive, resilient and globally operating companies . While early-stage innovation in manufacturing is thriving, many promising ventures struggle to move from pilot projects to large-scale production and international market leadership. The reasons are complex, ranging from access to growth capital and industrial infrastructure to regulatory environments, talent availability and collaboration between startups, corporates and the public sector. The event explores how different manufacturing ecosystems support or constrain this scale-up journey, and what enables some companies to grow faster, expand internationally and industrialise successfully. It looks at diverse investment models, policy frameworks and partnership approaches that shape how manufacturing innovation turns into industrial impact. Manufacturing Day 2026 brings together investors, founders, industry leaders and policymakers to identify the practical levers needed to accelerate manufacturing scale-ups: from financing and industrial capacity to market access and global expansion. The focus is on learning across systems, challenging assumptions, and defining what is needed to build the next generation of resilient, world-class manufacturing companies. What is Manufacturing Day? The Manufacturing Day is the leading event for the manufacturing industry during ViennaUP , the largest decentralised startup festival in Europe. During this landmark event with more than 500 participants from over 50 countries, EIT Manufacturing is bringing together startups, key industrial players, investors, researchers and experts to celebrate the achievements and potential of the European manufacturing industry. Renowned speakers from all over the world provide insights into hot topics in the industry, bringing new solutions and ideas to the table to make manufacturing more innovative and thus create a competitive advantage. The Manufacturing Day aims to showcase the latest innovations and technologies that are driving this transition and to inspire the next generation of manufacturing professionals to take an active role in shaping the future of the industry. The event offers a varied and rich programme for and with startups in the manufacturing industry and is a platform for industry representatives to exchange ideas and network. Save Manufacturing Day 2026 to your calendar Make sure to have 21 May 2026 reserved for Manufacturing Day during ViennaUP! Add to calendar Get ready for Manufacturing Day - coming up on 21 May 2026 in Vienna To get into the right mood, walk down memory lane with us and see some impressions from Manufacturing Day 2025 in the video below. We are working hard on creating an amazing programme with outstanding speakers for you. Save your spot and get your free ticket now! We are looking forward to seeing you on 21 May 2026 in Vienna. Your EIT Manufacturing East Team :) We welcome participants from all over the world! Be part of the largest innovation network for the manufacturing industry in Europe. Austria 361 Germany 35 Slovakia 22 Czech Republic 14 Türkiye 11 Poland 11 Italy 10 Hungary 10 India 9 Spain 7 United States 7 Serbia 7 Ukraine 6 Bulgaria 5 United Kingdom 4 Netherlands 4 Israel 4 Singapore 4 Romania 4 France 4 Switzerland 3 Greece 3 South Korea 2 Japan 2 Croatia 2 Ireland 2 Belgium 2 Kenya 2 China 2 Taiwan 1 Finland 1 South Africa 1 Bosnia And Herzegovina 1 Malaysia 1 Denmark 1 Norway 1 Morocco 1 Latvia 1 Cyprus 1 China (Hong Kong) 1 Estonia 1 Ghana 1 Tunisia 1 Malta 1 Côte d'Ivoire 1 Lithuania 1 Ethiopia 1 Bangladesh 1 Uganda 1 Thailand 1 Argentina 1 Total: 581 Who is behind the Manufacturing Day? Hello there, we are the team of EIT Manufacturing East, a group of experts that helps the manufacturing industry to become more innovative, more competitive, more resilient and more sustainable. Out of our office in Vienna, we work with startups, SMEs, large corporations, universities, research institutes and other professionals in the manufacturing sector on their innovation activities and challenges. We support them to find the right partners, solutions, technologies and talents for growing their businesses, expanding their markets, optimising their production and enhancing their efficiency and sustainability. We work with more than 1,000 European industry startups, we collaborate with top-notch universities and research institutes and we bring in all the big and small players from the business side along with the visible and the hidden champions to our innovation ecosystem. Together with the colleagues from our offices in Germany, Spain, Italy, Sweden, Greece and France, we follow a true European approach, enabling cooperations across company and national borders. EIT Manufacturing is the largest innovation network for manufacturing in Europe. Supported by the European Institute of Innovation & Technology (EIT), a body of the European Union, we bring together the leading industry stakeholders from business, academia and research. Fueled by a strong interdisciplinary and trusted community, our activities add unique value to European products, processes, and services – and inspire the creation of globally competitive and sustainable manufacturing. EIT Manufacturing is headquartered in Paris, France and has a network of innovation hubs across Europe: Vienna (Austria), Darmstadt (Germany), Milan (Italy), Gothenburg (Sweden), San Sebastian (Spain) and Athens (Greece). title: Creative Days Vienna 2026
descriptionFull:
How are digital technologies shaping the future of cultural experiences? On 20 and 21 May 2026, Creative Days Vienna will once again bring together leading international creative professionals for a diverse program of talks, tours, performances, installations and networking formats.

The participants of Creative Days Vienna 2026 will provide substantial impulses for architecture, design, film, gaming, fashion, music and the visual arts. Together, we will discuss the interplay between technological innovations, social change and the creative industries.

Creative Days Vienna 2026 will open with an (Un)Conference exploring how creatives can reshape cultural and digital infrastructures. The opening features keynotes by Shumon Basar and Günseli Yalcinkaya, as well as performances, installations and a club night. Day 2 brings workshops led by Paloma Oliveira, Ramon Reichert, NeverAtHome, Subotron, Marlène Delrive, Area 404 and a Tour presenting Biofabrique Vienna and Creatives for Vienna projects.

Let’s talk Kreativwirtschaft. Die internationale Kreativszene hat untersucht, wie digitale Technologien kulturelle Erfahrungen neu prägen. Bei den Creative Days Vienna 2026 drehten sich die Diskussionen um das Zusammenspiel von technologischer Innovation, gesellschaftlichem Wandel und den Kreativindustrien. Das Programm vereinte Perspektiven aus den Bereichen Architektur, Design, Film, Gaming, Mode, Musik und bildende Kunst. Es zeigte auf, wie digitale Infrastrukturen und Plattformen kreative Arbeit verändern. Eröffnet wurde die Veranstaltung am 20. Mai mit einer (Un)Conference im Funkhaus Wien , die sich damit befasste, wie Kreativschaffende kulturelle und digitale Infrastrukturen neu gestalten können. Beiträge von Nadia Piet (AIxDESIGN), Calum Bowden (trust) und Christie Morgan (SOFTER) griffen alternative Praktiken, kollaborative Systeme und neue Modelle für digitale Zukünfte auf. Die Content Vienna Screenings von Total View und Merve Sahin präsentierten Projekte, die mithilfe digitaler Werkzeuge verborgene Ebenen urbaner Räume und sozialer Strukturen sichtbar machen. Die Eröffnung wurde durch eine Keynote von Shumon Basar und einen Talk von Günseli Yalcinkaya abgerundet, begleitet von der interaktiven Installation AGENTIC EI von NODE Media Lab sowie einer Performance von Cat Jiménez, kuratiert von NeverAtHome , gefolgt von Networking und einer Clubnacht. Der zweite Tag konzentrierte sich auf Workshops und Austauschformate mit Paloma Oliveira und Kristina Maurer, Ramon Reichert mit NeverAtHome, Marlène Delrive und Georg Russegger mit SUBOTRON , Bika Rebek sowie Klimentina Milenova und Brooklyn J. Pakathi (AREA[404]). Die Sessions behandelten Themen wie neue Formen der Zusammenarbeit, digitale Infrastrukturen und sich wandelnde kreative Ökonomien. Ergänzt wurde das Programm durch eine Tour der Biofabrique Vienna und ausgewählten Projekten von Creatives for Vienna , die aktuelle Initiativen und räumliche Praktiken in der Stadt sichtbar machte. Die Creative Days Vienna 2026 zeigten, dass digitale Technologien nicht nur künstlerische Produktion und Distribution beeinflussen, sondern auch neue Möglichkeiten für Zusammenarbeit, Partizipation und kulturelle Infrastrukturen eröffnen. title: INSIDEOUT Summit
descriptionFull:
We're racing toward "faster, bigger, newer" — but building the future without upgrading the builders. 72% of innovators face mental health challenges. AI advances exponentially while human capacity stays unchanged.

INSIDEOUT Summit 2026 isn't just another conference. It's an immersive experience where 300+ founders, investors, and innovation leaders from 10+ countries explore what happens when inner growth drives outer impact.

Through keynotes, panels, lightning talks, a startup showcase, and formats like Lego Serious Play networking, you'll walk away with:

  • New perspectives on leading in a world that moves faster than we do
  • Real connections rooted in shared purpose, not business card exchanges
  • Practical tools for resilience and high performance as an innovator

Four themes. One day. A shift in how you build. Human 2.0 · Purposeful Innovation · Authentic Connection · Growth Unlocked

Ready to build the future from the inside out?

Includes access to all sessions and networking. 25,00 EUR Description We're racing toward "faster, bigger, newer" — but building the future without upgrading the builders. 72% of innovators face mental health challenges. AI advances exponentially while human capacity stays unchanged. INSIDEOUT Summit 2026 isn't another conference. It's an immersive experience where 300+ founders, investors, and innovation leaders from 10+ countries explore what happens when inner growth drives outer impact. Through keynotes, panels, lightning talks, a startup showcase, and formats like Lego Serious Play networking, you'll walk away with: New perspectives on leading in a world that moves faster than we do Real connections rooted in shared purpose, not business card exchanges Practical tools for resilience and high performance as an innovator Four themes. One day. A shift in how you build. Human 2.0 · Purposeful Innovation · Authentic Connection · Growth Unlocked Ready to build the future from the inside out? Agenda 09:30-10:30 - Registration & Breakfast - Doors open. Take your time to arrive, check in, and settle into the space. Pick up your badge, ease into the atmosphere, and start the day with relaxed, meaningful conversations. Enjoy a warm breakfast powered by Purora - a simple, comforting start designed to bring people together before the day unfolds. 10:30-10:50 - Opening - We begin by coming together. Opening remarks by our co-host partner Mastercard Austria and hosting partner weXelerate set the tone for the day, welcoming you into the experience, sharing what lies ahead, and inviting you to be fully part of the conversations to come. Speaker: Eleftherios Kousakis - Vice President, Account Management, Mastercard 10:50-11:15 - Keynote: Thriving Through Uncertainty - Pressure, change and uncertainty are increasing - and it's hard to sustain your thinking and performance under these conditions. In this session you'll learn five evidence-based domains that elite performers use to maintain clarity, focus, and impact when everything feels unstable. Learn how to regain your sense of control, and build the habits and skills to sustain your decision making, energy and performance through pressure, change and uncertainty. Practical tips, grounded in neuroscience and psychological research to consistently perform at your best. Speaker: Phil Dobson - Founder, BrainWorkshops 11:15-11:50 - Lightning Talks - Five ideas. Five voices. Five short moments that might stay with you longer than expected. Powered by FFG, this session brings together a curated lineup of innovators presenting solutions that are actively shaping a better world, from bold concepts to real, funded initiatives already in motion. Expect a series of 5-minute lightning talks: concise, inspiring stories that offer a glimpse into what’s possible when innovation meets purpose Speakers: Kris Hofmann - Creative Director, Vox Body Armin Knöbl - Chairman, Silosophie Gabriella Chihan Stanley - Founder & Team Lead, Shrine Tayebeh Saghaei - Founder, SpinCell Stefan Wöhrer - CEO, OWNER and System Developer, HOMIT Verkehrswegebau FlexCo 11:50- 12:20 - Panel: “Humanizing Impact Finance” - As part of INSIDEOUT Summit’s core theme - “Humanizing Innovation” - this panel explores the idea that meaningful, lasting impact begins with people, not just capital or products. “Humanizing Impact Finance” brings together voices from across the ecosystem to discuss how financial decisions can be guided by empathy, ethics, and real human needs. From investors to founders, the conversation will highlight how purpose-driven capital can create sustainable change, why human-centered approaches matter more than ever, and how impact finance can move beyond numbers to truly transform communities. Speakers: Margit Mayr - Impact Investment Manager, Erste Social Finance Holding Johann Heep - Social Banking Expert at Erste Group Social Banking Wolfgang Weingraber - CEO & Co-Founder, byeagain GmbH Moderator: Petra Capin - Co-founder, tech2impact 12.20-12:40 - Coffee break - Grab a cup and recharge for what’s next. A great moment to reflect - or meet someone new. 12:40-13:30 - Roundtable Discussions: Where Ecosystems meet - Collaboration in Practice - Collaboration sounds great on stage. In practice, when startups, corporates, funders, researchers, and humanitarian organizations come together, it can often be slower and more complex than expected, with different expectations, timelines and ways of working. This roundtable, hosted by REDpreneur (the Austrian Red Cross’ global social innovation program), brings together 5–6 parallel table conversations with people from across the system. Whether you are a founder, funder, corporate, operator, or researcher, we invite you to join a real exchange on what makes collaboration work, where it breaks, and how innovation grounded in real human needs, humanitarian work included, can emerge when different worlds succeed in connecting. No pitching. No panels. Just a few table, a few questions, and the kind of conversation you usually only get over dinner. 12:40-13:30 - Moderated Networking - LEGO® Serious Play® - A playful, moderated networking session using LEGO® Serious Play®, designed to spark meaningful connections, fresh perspectives, and engaging conversations in an interactive and inspiring way. Moderator: Sabine Reitmayer-Wawer - Business-, Conflict- & Teamcoach | LEGO® Serious Play® Facilitator | Owner, E61 GmbH | Sabine Reitmayer-Wawer 13:30-14:30 - Lunch break - Time to recharge, connect with fellow participants, and spark new conversations in a relaxed atmosphere. 14:30-15:00 - Keynote “Fintech Is Everyone's Business Now” - powered by Mastercard , exploring new pathways to growth and innovation. Speaker: Andreas Spengel - Senior Vice President, Digital Partners, Central Europe - Mastercard 15:00- 15:30 - Panel discussion “The Funding Reality Check”, powered by Mastercard , featuring insights on investment trends and scaling ventures. In this fireside chat, two founders take the stage to share the journeys behind what we often simply call “success.” Through personal stories, pivotal decisions, and lessons learned along the way, they offer a more human perspective on building something meaningful. Expect real insights, honest reflections, and moments that go beyond the highlight reel. Speakers: Michael Wüst, CEO of Zen Friedrich Hubel - COO & Managing Director, Pliant GmbH Moderator: Alexandra Love, Mastercard 16:00-16:30 - Coffee Break - A perfect moment to recharge, refresh, and connect with fellow participants. 16:30-17:30 - Mastermind: Thriving Through Uncertainty - This 50-minute interactive session goes beyond habits to build genuine capacity for navigating pressure, change and uncertainty.
